question: What is the definition of the abbreviation RCS?

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, detailing the symbols and descriptions for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices. It includes representations for sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, and level indicators, ensuring standardized interpretation of P&ID drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0786.title: DSRS 15.6.6, Rev 0: Inadvertent Operation of ECCSII.1 RCS pressure below 110 percent design value.
nuscale_ch01_0786.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding the inadvertent operation of ECCS at RCS pressures below 110% of the design value. It concludes that NuScale fully conforms to the relevant DSRS 15.6.6 requirements, with no exceptions noted for RCS pressure and minimum DNBR criteria. Some related SRP guidance is only partially applicable or not applicable, particularly where it pertains to BWRs or is superseded by other sections.

nuscale_ch01_0701.title: DSRS 15.1.6, Rev 0: Loss of Containment VacuumII.4 Analytical ParametersMaximize Heat Transfer from RCS to Containment and Reactor Pool
nuscale_ch01_0701.summary: This section documents NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.1.6, specifically regarding analytical parameters for the loss of containment vacuum scenario. It confirms that the analysis maximizes heat transfer from the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) to the containment and reactor pool, and that all relevant parameters conform to regulatory requirements. No exceptions or deviations are noted for this criterion.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis report diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system status and control operations, ensuring clarity in interpreting the technical drawings. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and numbering of text, tables, figures, and pages within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), following specific regulatory guidance. It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure and is clearly marked, with a separate public version provided that excludes such material.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used for identifying and numbering tables, figures, and pages within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. It clarifies that sensitive content is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements, while all such material is clearly marked and included only in the non-public version.

edges.description: Section B uses the abbreviation 'RCS' in the context of regulatory criteria, while Section A provides diagram legends that may define such abbreviations, thus Section A potentially specifies the meaning of 'RCS' referenced in Section B.

Section B uses the abbreviation 'RCS' in the context of analytical parameters, while Section A is a legend for diagrams that likely defines such abbreviations, thus Section A provides the definition for 'RCS' as used in Section B.

Section A provides general information about acronyms and abbreviations in the FSAR, while Section B contains diagram legends that may use such abbreviations, connecting them through the use and explanation of abbreviations like RCS.

Section A provides a list of acronyms and abbreviations, which is where the definition of 'RCS' would be found, while Section B discusses technical and regulatory criteria related to RCS but does not define the abbreviation itself.

Section B uses the abbreviation 'RCS' without definition, while Section A is titled 'Acronyms and Abbreviations' and is expected to provide definitions for such abbreviations, thus Section A is intended to specify the meaning of 'RCS' as used in Section B.
Section B is expected to provide the definitions of acronyms and abbreviations such as RCS, which is the information sought in the analysis question, while Section A does not address abbreviations.
